title: Seseli fasciculatum
intro: Seseli fasciculatum, also known as clustered sesame, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, with its health benefits attributed to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Seseli fasciculatum include sesquiterpenes, such as seselin and apiol, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Seseli fasciculatum is commonly used in herbal preparations, including essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, often employed to alleviate respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Seseli fasciculatum belongs to the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification indicates that Seseli fasciculatum is a member of the order Apiales. The genus Seseli is a group of flowering plants that are native to various parts of the world. The species fasciculatum is a specific type within the genus Seseli, characterized by its unique characteristics. The classification of Seseli fasciculatum as a member of the Apiaceae family suggests its close relationship with other plants in the same family.
